metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify explicitly how `author_criterion_overlay` (or its caller) obtains the dotted criterion id `project.foo` rather than deriving it by stripping the sanitized prompt id prefix — e.g. change its signature to accept `criterion_id` directly (the caller knows it), or define and use an inverse `criterion_id_from_prompt_id` that handles the `project.` namespace case, so the overlay routing key is always the dotted `project.<name>` form the merge logic requires.
findings.finding: The mechanism for `author_criterion_overlay` receiving the correct dotted criterion id (`project.foo`) is underspecified: the existing implementation derives `cid` by stripping the `plan-review-` prefix from `prompt_id`, so with the sanitized prompt id `plan-review-project-foo` it would produce `project-foo` (dash) — a non-`project.`-prefixed id that the overlay merge rejects as a net-new non-namespaced criterion, causing a 400 on every new-criterion authoring attempt.
findings.location: What section — 'Route BOTH sides through it'; `src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py` line 535 (`author_criterion_overlay`)
findings.suggested_fix: Change `author_criterion_overlay`'s signature to accept the criterion id directly (`criterion_id: str`) rather than deriving it from `prompt_id`; update `editor.py` to pass the dotted criterion id (e.g. split from the prompt id via `criterion_prompt_id`'s inverse, or by having the editor send the criterion id as a separate field). This makes the overlay write key fully explicit and independent of the prompt-id derivation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `author_criterion_overlay` recovers the dotted `project.<name>` criterion id from the sanitized `plan-review-project-<name>` prompt id (e.g., add an inverse `criterion_id_from_prompt_id` function or change the function signature to accept the criterion id directly rather than the prompt id).
findings.finding: `author_criterion_overlay` (criterion_preview.py:535) derives the criterion id (`cid`) by stripping the literal `'plan-review-'` prefix from `prompt_id`. After the plan's change, the editor writes the rubric as prompt id `'plan-review-project-foo'` (via `criterion_prompt_id`). Stripping `'plan-review-'` yields `'project-foo'` (hyphenated), NOT `'project.foo'` (dotted), so `write_criterion_overlay` would record a wrong/invalid key in `criteria_routing.json`. The plan does not specify an inverse mapping or update to this stripping logic.
findings.location: Scope section: 'editor authoring: criterion_preview.author_criterion_overlay writes the rubric via create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), ...) ... then the atomic overlay routing+activate write keyed by the dotted project.<name> id'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `editor.py` to the Scope edit list and specify that `_library_create` must call `create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), ...)` (not the raw `pid`) when `kind == 'criterion'`.
findings.finding: `editor.py::_library_create` is an unenumerated edit target that calls `create_prompt(pid, text, repo_root=_repo_root)` with the raw user-supplied id, then calls `author_criterion_overlay(str(_repo_root), pid, routing)` — both calls must use the `criterion_prompt_id`-mapped form for criteria, yet `editor.py` does not appear anywhere in the plan's Scope section.
findings.location: Scope section; `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` lines 712–744
findings.suggested_fix: In `editor.py::_library_create`, after computing `pid` (the criterion logical id, e.g. `project.foo`), derive the prompt id via `from rebar.llm.criteria.model import criterion_prompt_id; prompt_pid = criterion_prompt_id(pid) if kind == 'criterion' else pid` and pass `prompt_pid` to `create_prompt`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan (and implement) how `author_criterion_overlay` recovers the logical dotted id from the sanitized prompt id — either change its signature to accept the logical `criterion_id` directly, or add an inverse mapping `prompt_id_to_criterion_id` alongside `criterion_prompt_id`.
findings.finding: The plan describes `author_criterion_overlay` as the function that "writes the rubric via `create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), ...)`" and "then the atomic overlay routing+activate write keyed by the dotted `project.<name>` id", but the current `author_criterion_overlay` (line 535) derives the criterion id by stripping the `plan-review-` prefix from whatever `prompt_id` it receives — after the fix this yields `project-foo` (dashes), not `project.foo` (dot). The plan does not specify whether `author_criterion_overlay`'s signature changes to accept a logical criterion id or whether it must apply the inverse mapping internally.
findings.location: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py` lines 521–551; What section ('atomic overlay routing+activate write keyed by the dotted `project.<name>` id')
findings.suggested_fix: Change `author_criterion_overlay`'s parameter from `prompt_id` to `criterion_id` (the logical dotted id) and have it call `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` internally when it needs the prompt-library id; the overlay write already uses `criterion_id` directly for the routing key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the inverse of `criterion_prompt_id` as a co-located, named function (e.g., `criterion_id_from_prompt_id`) and document that `author_criterion_overlay` must use it (not the naive prefix-strip) to recover `'project.foo'` from `'plan-review-project-foo'`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the forward mapping `criterion_prompt_id(cid)` but omits the inverse protocol needed by `author_criterion_overlay`, which currently derives `cid` by naively stripping the literal `'plan-review-'` prefix from `prompt_id`. After the plan's change, `prompt_id` will be `'plan-review-project-foo'`; stripping `'plan-review-'` yields `'project-foo'`, not `'project.foo'`. The existing validate-then-rollback will then reject this as a non-`project.`-prefixed net-new id — the same 400 dead-end the plan claims to fix.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py:535 (`author_criterion_overlay`); plan section 'What — decouple the logical id from the physical filename'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `criterion_id_from_prompt_id(prompt_id) -> str` next to `criterion_prompt_id`: strip `'plan-review-project-'` → prepend `'project.'`, else strip `'plan-review-'` (built-in). Update `author_criterion_overlay` to use it instead of the bare `prompt_id[len('plan-review-'):]` strip.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `production_batch_runner._resolve_criteria` to the Scope edit list and specify that the prefix-strip logic must apply `criterion_prompt_id`'s inverse (prompt id → logical id) so `plan-review-project-foo` resolves to `project.foo` in `by_id()`.
findings.finding: `production_batch_runner._resolve_criteria` (line 177–178) strips the `plan-review-` prefix from a batch criterion's `prompt` field to obtain the registry key. After the fix, a project criterion's prompt id becomes `plan-review-project-foo` (dashes), but stripping `plan-review-` yields `project-foo` — a key not present in `registry.by_id()` (which is keyed by the logical id `project.foo`). This unenumerated consumer silently skips project criteria in batch execution.
findings.location: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py` lines 177–191; Scope section (not listed)
findings.suggested_fix: After stripping `plan-review-`, additionally check if the result starts with `project-` (from the sanitized form) and map it back to the logical id via the inverse of `criterion_prompt_id` (replace the first `-` after `project` with `.`), or expose an inverse `criterion_id_from_prompt_id` helper alongside `criterion_prompt_id`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `production_batch_runner.py`'s `_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX`-strip normalization (lines 177-178) to the Scope as a required edit site: it must use `criterion_id_from_prompt_id` (or the equivalent inverse of `criterion_prompt_id`) instead of the bare `_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX` strip.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope omits `production_batch_runner.py` lines 177-178, which perform a separate `_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX`-strip normalization on criterion ids. This code path must also understand the new mapping for project criteria: a batch `criteria` entry whose `prompt` is `'plan-review-project-foo'` would strip `'plan-review-'` to `'project-foo'` and fail `registry.by_id()` lookup (no descriptor found → skipped with a warning). This consumer of the `prompt_id → cid` mapping is not covered by the plan.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py:177-178; plan section 'Scope'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update ADR 0015 (or add an ADR amendment / new ADR) to document the `criterion_prompt_id` mapping contract and the rationale for decoupling logical id from physical filename, so the architectural decision is traceable.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural contract — `criterion_prompt_id` as the sole canonical id→filename mapping — and changes the filename convention implied by ef7e/ADR 0015, but does not mention updating ADR 0015 or adding any doc/ADR entry for the new decoupling pattern.
findings.location: What section — 'criterion_prompt_id' canonical function; Scope — 'Files' list; Why — 'ef7e/ADR 0015' reference
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short ADR amendment section to ADR 0015 (or a new ADR 0016): 'The project.<name> logical criterion id maps to the rubric filename via criterion_prompt_id: project.<name> → plan-review-project-<name>. Rationale: prompt_library._valid_id forbids dots in prompt ids (filename-safety); decoupling follows the Semgrep/npm/Python pattern. The name component is constrained to [A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]* (no dots).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the `criterion_prompt_id` mapping contract (id→prompt-filename decoupling, charset constraint, injectivity guarantee) in ADR 0015 (or a companion ADR/CLAUDE.md section) so future contributors know it is the mandatory single derivation point.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural contract — `criterion_prompt_id()` as the single, canonical id→prompt-filename derivation — that crosses five components (model.py/ids.py, registry.py, criterion_preview.py, overlay.py, rebarProvider.jsx), yet it does not call for updating ADR 0015, CLAUDE.md, or any design doc to capture this new mapping layer.
findings.location: ## What — decouple the logical id from the physical filename / Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to ADR 0015 (or a new ADR 0016) titled 'Criterion id → prompt filename mapping' that records: (1) the `criterion_prompt_id` function is the sole derivation; (2) built-in `<id>` → `plan-review-<id>`, project `project.<name>` → `plan-review-project-<name>`; (3) project names must match `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`; (4) no dotted fallback exists. Reference this ADR in a docstring on `criterion_prompt_id`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'Verification' section (or annotate each AC) with a concrete proving command for each criterion — e.g., `pytest tests/llm/criteria/test_criterion_prompt_id.py -v` for AC 1/3/4, a named integration test file/command for AC 2, and the explicit command for AC 5.
findings.finding: No concrete proving commands are specified anywhere in the plan; acceptance criteria describe what tests should prove but name no test file, test class, or CLI invocation that an agent can run to generate evidence of completion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs)
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a section like:
```
## Verification
- AC 1/3/4: `pytest tests/llm/criteria/test_criterion_prompt_id.py -v` (unit tests for mapping, injectivity, dot-in-name error)
- AC 2: `pytest tests/llm/integration/test_criterion_round_trip.py -v` (author→activate→load→run integration test)
- AC 5: `pytest tests/llm/editor/test_criterion_preview.py -v` covering the 400-rejection fix; document UI verification rationale if browser test is out of scope.
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope edit target for rubric resolution: the change needed is in the `_get_prompt` closure at `registry.py:233-236` (replace `f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}"` with `f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)}"`), NOT in `build_descriptor` itself.
findings.finding: The plan names `criteria.model.build_descriptor` as an edit target for rubric resolution of project criteria, but `build_descriptor` is a pure transformer that never constructs prompt ids — it receives an injected `prompt_getter` callable. The actual prompt-id construction site is the `_get_prompt` closure in `registry.py:233-236` (`f"{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}"`). Naming the wrong function as the edit site will lead an implementer to modify the wrong layer.
findings.location: Scope section: 'descriptor resolution: criteria.model.build_descriptor / plan_review.registry resolve a project criterion's rubric via get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), repo_root=...)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, replace 'criteria.model.build_descriptor' with 'the _get_prompt closure inside plan_review.registry._descriptor_from_prompt (registry.py:233-236)' as the edit target for rubric resolution.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the edit site for rubric resolution is `registry._descriptor_from_prompt`'s inner `_get_prompt` closure (line 236), not `build_descriptor`, and specify that `_get_prompt` must call `get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id), ...)` instead of `f'{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}'`.
findings.finding: The plan identifies `criteria.model.build_descriptor` as the edit site for 'descriptor rubric resolution via `get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), ...)`', but `build_descriptor` is a pure transformer that never constructs a prompt id. The actual prompt-id construction is in `registry._descriptor_from_prompt`'s `_get_prompt` closure (line 236: `prompts.get_prompt(f'{_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX}{criterion_id}', repo_root=root)`). This is the only call site that constructs `plan-review-<cid>` and passes it to `get_prompt` — the plan's stated fix must land here, not in `build_descriptor`.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:233-236 (`_descriptor_from_prompt._get_prompt`); plan section 'Scope'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For AC 5, either add an end-to-end / integration test that exercises the full editor→API→file path (e.g., an API-level test that POSTs a `project.<name>` criterion and confirms `create_prompt` succeeds and the overlay is written), or document an explicit rationale for why browser-level testing is out of scope for this story.
findings.finding: AC 5 (editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id; net-new author no longer dead-ends at a 400) is a user-facing flow with no end-to-end check described and no rationale given for its absence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 5; Scope — 'the editor UI (editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC #2) that verifies the old dotted fixture filename (`plan-review-project.no_print.md` or equivalent) no longer appears in the test suite and that the canonical sanitized filename is used by all tests exercising project criterion resolution.
findings.finding: The Scope section describes a concrete deliverable — rewriting test fixtures that currently hand-place a dotted `plan-review-project.no_print.md` to the sanitized `plan-review-project-no-print.md` form — but no Acceptance Criterion covers this cleanup, leaving described work unverified.
findings.location: Scope section — 'the ef7e/overlay TEST FIXTURES that hand-place a dotted plan-review-project.no_print.md are just rewritten to the sanitized project.no-print → plan-review-project-no-print.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC #2: 'The existing test fixtures previously using the dotted filename `plan-review-project.<name>.md` are updated to `plan-review-project-<name>.md`; no dotted project-criterion prompt filename remains in the test corpus.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the editor form's inline validation behavior for invalid project names (disallowed characters like `.` or `_`): define whether validation is client-side (with an error message / disabled submit) or server-side, and ensure the form surface matches the charset constraint in AC3.
findings.finding: The editor form's behavior for an invalid project name (containing dots, underscores, or other disallowed characters) is not specified — the plan says it is 'a located error at overlay load' (server-side), leaving the UX for inline validation/error display in the authoring form unaddressed.
findings.location: Scope / editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx; AC3; AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Add a client-side regex check in rebarProvider.jsx that validates the name field against [A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]* and shows an inline error (e.g., 'Name may only contain letters, digits, and hyphens') with the submit button disabled until valid. This closes the loop between AC3 (charset constraint) and AC5 (editor form UX).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a startup/load-time guard (or one-shot rename utility) that detects stale dotted project-criterion rubric files (matching `plan-review-project.*.md`) in `.rebar/prompts/` and emits a located error or auto-migrates them, so the 'no back-compat burden' assumption is validated at runtime rather than silently breaking existing users.
findings.finding: The plan changes the persisted filename format for project-criterion rubric files (from `plan-review-project.name.md` to `plan-review-project-name.md`) but provides no detection, warning, or one-shot migration for any stale dotted files that may already exist in a real `.rebar/prompts/` directory. The 'no external data/users' assertion is unverified; if any repo created a project criterion during the ef7e window, its rubric file silently becomes invisible to the new resolver (no error, no fallback, no migration path).
findings.location: Scope section — 'No back-compat burden (just-shipped feature)' paragraph; AC line 2 (author→activate→load→run round trip)
findings.suggested_fix: In `criterion_prompt_id` resolution (or in `enumerate_library` / `load_criteria`), scan for filenames matching `plan-review-project.*.md`; if any are found, raise a `ConfigurationError` that names the file and instructs the user to rename it to the sanitized form (e.g., `plan-review-project-foo.md`). This costs ~5 lines and makes the 'no users' assumption safe to act on.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Collapse AC #1 and AC #4 into a single criterion (or clearly differentiate them): move the 'underscored project name is a located error' assertion into AC #3 (charset constraint) and remove the redundant restatement of the mapping and 'no dotted fallback' from AC #4.
findings.finding: AC #1 and AC #4 are near-duplicate requirements: both assert that `criterion_prompt_id` is the single/only id→prompt-filename derivation with no dotted fallback and the same `project.<name>`→`plan-review-project-<name>` mapping. The only incremental content in AC #4 ('total + injective,' 'dotted/underscored project name is a located error') is already partially covered by AC #1 ('ONLY id→prompt-filename derivation') and AC #3 (dot-free constraint). This creates redundant acceptance surface that an implementer may satisfy twice or conflate.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #1 and AC #4
findings.suggested_fix: Merge: keep AC #1 as the canonical 'one mapping, used by both sides' criterion; extend AC #3 to also prohibit underscores ('dot-free AND underscore-free charset, one namespace dot'); delete AC #4 or replace it with a distinct testable property not already in AC #1/#3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Drop the `ids.py` option from scope and commit to placing `criterion_prompt_id` in the existing `criteria/model.py` (where `_PROMPT_ID_PREFIX`, `CriteriaError`, and `build_descriptor` already live), to avoid a premature single-function module.
findings.finding: The plan proposes `ids.py` as an alternative home for `criterion_prompt_id` — a new shared module for a single ~4-line function — when `criteria/model.py` already exists, already hosts the shared criteria machinery, and is the natural location. Creating a new module here would be premature module-level extraction with no justification beyond naming preference.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py (or a small shared ids.py)'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the `(or a small shared ids.py)` parenthetical from the Scope section; the Scope bullet should read only `src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py — criterion_prompt_id`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the dot-free project-name charset check will be applied to the `cid` argument of `_validate_routing_entry` (or at the `_effective_routing_cached` cid-iteration loop), and specify the exact regex (e.g., `project.<name>` where `<name>` matches `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`) so the implementer doesn't have to infer it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `_validate_routing_entry` (or 'the namespace check') currently enforces or is the right place to add a dot-free charset constraint on project names. Reading the actual `_validate_routing_entry` implementation (overlay.py:130-175) confirms it does NOT currently validate the `<name>` portion's charset — it only checks structural fields (`exec`, `block_threshold`, `default_posture`, `fail_mode`, `disabled`). While adding it there is feasible, the plan presents this as a straightforward 'add constraint' without noting that the cid itself (the key in the routing JSON) would need to be validated, not just its fields.
findings.location: Scope section: 'overlay.py or _validate_routing_entry (constrain project name to dot-free)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope: 'In `_validate_routing_entry`, when `cid.startswith(_PROJECT_PREFIX)`, validate that the suffix (`cid[len(_PROJECT_PREFIX):]`) matches `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`; raise a located CriteriaError naming the file if not.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out step to the plan (e.g., 'revert this PR and re-run the dotted-filename convention; no migration script is needed because the feature is <1 week old and no user-facing data exists') so reviewers and on-call engineers know the reversibility path.
findings.finding: The plan changes the physical storage schema for project-criterion prompts (dotted filenames → dashed filenames) and removes the old path entirely ('one clean code path, no dotted-get_prompt fallback'), but states no explicit rollback or back-out mechanism. T4 requires a reversibility mechanism, not just a justified acknowledgement of the break.
findings.location: Scope — 'No back-compat burden (just-shipped feature)' paragraph; AC #4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line 'Rollback' section: 'Back-out: revert this PR. Because no external users or persisted user data exist for project criteria (feature landed in ef7e, days ago), no data migration or undo script is required; the prior dotted-filename convention is restored by the revert alone.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add accessibility done-definitions to the editor UI scope: specify that the derived criterion-id display uses an `aria-live` region (or equivalent) so screen readers announce the derived value, all new form controls are keyboard-reachable, and contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA SC 1.4.3; include these as observable a11y acceptance criteria for the `rebarProvider.jsx` change.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a modified user-facing editor UI (`editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx`) that surfaces the resulting criterion id to the author, but contains zero accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) provisions — no keyboard navigation, screen-reader labeling, contrast, or reduced-motion considerations are mentioned for this new/modified interactive surface.
findings.location: Scope — 'the editor UI (editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx) so the authoring form derives + shows the resulting criterion id'; AC #5
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC #5: 'The derived criterion id display is implemented as or wrapped in an `aria-live="polite"` region so assistive technology announces the computed value; all new/modified form controls are reachable and activatable via keyboard alone (WCAG 2.1 AA SC 2.1.1); text contrast meets SC 1.4.3.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify atomicity across the full two-phase write (rubric file + routing): define whether create_prompt is idempotent on retry, document the cleanup/rollback path if the routing write fails after create_prompt succeeds, and confirm no orphaned rubric files can accumulate.
findings.finding: The rubric file write (create_prompt) and the overlay routing+activate write are two separate steps with no atomic commit across both. If create_prompt succeeds but the routing write fails, an orphaned rubric file accumulates under .rebar/prompts/ with no retire/cleanup path specified.
findings.location: Scope / criterion_preview.author_criterion_overlay description; AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Make the authoring sequence explicitly idempotent: if create_prompt always overwrites and the routing write is atomic, a retry is safe. Document this contract. Alternatively, write the routing entry first (in a 'pending' state), then the rubric, then activate — so a crash before the rubric write leaves a non-activated entry that a retry can safely overwrite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the prior-art justification in the 'What' section: the Semgrep/npm/Python analogies illustrate *logical-vs-physical decoupling in principle* but none uses flat dot→dash substitution in a single directory — note this gap explicitly and justify the flat-stem choice on its own merits (e.g., the prompt store is already a flat dir, a subdirectory would require `get_prompt` / `_user_prompt_ids` changes that are out of scope).
findings.finding: The three cited prior-art analogies (Semgrep, npm, Python) all decouple logical id from physical path via directory hierarchy or pure metadata — none uses dot→dash substitution in a flat single directory. Python's `a.b.c → a/b/c.py` and npm's `@scope/name → node_modules/@scope/name/` create directory nesting; Semgrep decouples via a metadata field with the file path being irrelevant. The plan's chosen mechanism (flat `.rebar/prompts/` dir, single-stem `plan-review-project-<name>.md`) is a valid design but is not actually demonstrated or validated by the cited analogies, making the justification imprecise.
findings.location: ## What — decouple the logical id from the physical filename (the Semgrep/npm/Python pattern)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a clarifying sentence after the three analogies: 'The analogues show that decoupling is established practice; our specific mechanism — flat-directory dot→dash substitution — is the simplest mapping compatible with the existing flat `.rebar/prompts/` store and avoids introducing directory structure into `get_prompt` / `_user_prompt_ids`.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
